story: After Kanos barrels into the H.A.M. headquarters, he finds Ivy lying gray with blood, Daniel grinning among chanting men. Daniel and the H.A.M. crew murdered Ivy. Kanos loses control and kills them—the twenty humans later tied to his conviction were those H.A.M. men. He keeps vigil over Ivy for twelve hours; he tears off her left hand only to take the wedding ring, a final keepsake. Later, with Kanos in chains, reporters mass on the courthouse steps, microphones raised, waiting for answers.
implausible_knowledge: Daniel and the H.A.M. crew murdered Ivy.the twenty humans later tied to his conviction were those H.A.M. men.he tears off her left hand only to take the wedding ring, a final keepsake.
question: When Lauren corners Kanos on the courthouse steps, what line of questioning will she pursue?
answer_with_knowledge: Confront the H.A.M. angle by asking about Daniel’s role in Ivy’s murder and invite Kanos to explain the ring, challenging the official narrative that he killed her.
answer_without_knowledge: Grill Kanos with accusatory, sensational questions implying he killed Ivy and took her left hand as a trophy or ritual, and press him about the twenty dead humans.
